findings.finding: design-critic technical-1: INT-deny-response.permissionDecisionReason retains changelog language Updated to reference the bypass mechanism instead of plugin disablement instead of an implementable content contract
findings.match_reason: GAP-15 specified brief denial reason referencing bypass mechanism, but the actual artifact text reads Updated to reference the bypass mechanism instead of plugin disablement — changelog phrasing. No gap describes this residual authoring artifact in the interface field.

findings.finding: technical-critic technical-critic-1: CMP-block-skill-internals responsibility Pattern-match tool input paths against protected file names does not specify that path extraction requires checking different JSON fields per tool (file_path for Read, pattern for Glob, path for Grep)
findings.match_reason: GAP-10 added Glob/Grep bypass scenarios. GAP-22 and GAP-32 added per-tool payload formats to infra. But the technical component responsibility text itself still omits the multi-field extraction contract.

findings.finding: requirements-critic REQUIREMENTS-1: SCN-bypass-not-set and SCN-bypass-empty-string each contain a second then-clause asserting denial message content, coupling REQ-env-var-bypass and CAP-actionable-denial assertions in a single scenario
findings.match_reason: GAP-9 split SCN-denial-shows-bypass-instructions but explicitly did not address these two scenarios. Both still carry a second then-clause about denial message content.

findings.finding: requirements-critic REQUIREMENTS-2: REQ-updated-denial-message rule body retains to bypass the hook for the session — internal implementation terminology
findings.match_reason: GAP-29 removed systemMessage from the rule. GAP-26 removed hook from user-impact. Neither addressed the hook in bypass the hook for the session in the rule body itself.

findings.finding: requirements-coverage-critic REQUIREMENTS-COVERAGE-1: No scenario verifies that Grep is denied when FSM_BYPASS is not set; SCN-bypass-active-grep only verifies the allow path
findings.match_reason: GAP-10 added bypass-only Grep scenarios. No resolved gap adds a Grep denial scenario.

findings.finding: validation-critic REQUIREMENTS-COVERAGE-1: CAP-actionable-denial denial message verification exists only for Glob scenarios; no Grep denial scenario verifies actionable message for the distinct tool_input.path code branch
findings.match_reason: Overlaps with requirements-coverage-critic finding but frames it as CAP-actionable-denial coverage gap. No resolved gap addresses absence of Grep denial scenario under either capability.

findings.finding: verification-critic TESTABILITY-1: SCN-bypass-active-grep payload uses directory path /path/to/skills/ which may not match any blocked file pattern, making bypass assertion trivially pass
findings.match_reason: GAP-32 corrected field names but explicitly did not address whether the directory path triggers blocking. Infra artifact confirms directory path in SCN-bypass-active-grep payload.

findings.finding: design-for-test-critic INFRA-1: Manual test procedure payloads omit tool_name field present in real PreToolUse hook invocations; if hook routes on tool_name, payloads would not reproduce real invocations
findings.match_reason: No resolved gap addresses whether hook script uses tool_name for routing. GAP-2, GAP-14, GAP-22, GAP-32 refined payloads but none documented presence or absence of tool_name.

findings.finding: code-tasks-critic tasks-critic-1: TSK-update-denial-message applies unified language to both fields, obscuring that INT-deny-response makes systemMessage and permissionDecisionReason qualitatively different
findings.match_reason: GAP-15 differentiated the two fields in INT-deny-response. GAP-3 and GAP-34 modified TSK-verify-bypass (verification task), not TSK-update-denial-message (implementation task). No gap targeted TSK-update-denial-message description.

findings.finding: test-tasks-critic TASKS-1: TSK-verify-bypass denial scenarios 5-8 omit exit code assertion; bypass scenarios 1-4 explicitly state exits 0 empty stdout but denial scenarios only state deny JSON emitted
findings.match_reason: GAP-3 added content verification. GAP-34 enumerated all 8 scenarios. Neither added exit code assertions to denial scenarios. Infra testing-strategy requires hook exits 0 for denial scenarios.

findings.finding: test-infra-critic TASKS-INFRA-1: TSK-verify-bypass deny JSON emitted shorthand does not mandate the three-field structural verification that infra testing-strategy explicitly requires
findings.match_reason: GAP-3, GAP-16, GAP-34 all addressed TSK-verify-bypass content but none added structural JSON field verification. Infra requires stdout contains JSON with decision, systemMessage, and permissionDecisionReason fields.

findings.finding: technical-1: DEC-null-component-interactions cites an external mitigation with no cross-reference
findings.match_reason: GAP-20 resolved by adding DEC-null-component-interactions with a Y-statement, but no gap addresses the concern that the decision's stated mitigant (an integration section comment) is not located or cross-referenced within the decision itself.

findings.finding: technical-2: Context cites a spec artifact introduced by this change as pre-existing evidence
findings.match_reason: GAP-28 resolved by extending technical context, and its Decision text contains the exact sentence the finding critiques. No gap covers the conflation of newly introduced artifacts with pre-existing context.

findings.finding: REQUIREMENTS-1: SCN-bypass-not-set Given overlaps with SCN-bypass-empty-string
findings.match_reason: GAP-13 added SCN-bypass-empty-string but never narrowed SCN-bypass-not-set Given which still reads FSM_BYPASS is not set or is empty. No gap records narrowing the Given.

findings.finding: REQUIREMENTS-2: REQ-updated-denial-message rule lacks a normative negative constraint
findings.match_reason: GAP-18/25/29/42 updated language but none add a MUST NOT clause to the rule body. SCN-denial-removes-plugin-advice asserts behavior not mandated by any rule.

findings.finding: SCENARIO-1: No Read bypass scenario for fsm.json file pattern
findings.match_reason: GAP-11 added hooks.json bypass. GAP-10 added Glob/Grep bypass. No gap covers a Read bypass scenario for fsm.json specifically.

findings.finding: testability-1: SCN-bypass-not-set Given overlaps with SCN-bypass-empty-string precondition
findings.match_reason: Identical concern to REQUIREMENTS-1. GAP-13 never updated SCN-bypass-not-set Given to remove or is empty.

findings.finding: infra-1: Script path missing from manual test procedure
findings.match_reason: GAP-2/14/22/32/33/40 refined payloads but none address specifying the repository path to block-skill-internals.sh.

findings.finding: infra-2: No tooling specified for JSON structural verification
findings.match_reason: GAP-2 added stdout criteria. GAP-44 added presence preamble. Neither names a tool for structural JSON verification in the infra spec.

findings.finding: tasks-critic-1: decision field value not verified in denial scenarios
findings.match_reason: GAP-44 added presence-only preamble. GAP-15 established decision as Always deny. No gap requires asserting decision == deny as a value check.

findings.finding: tasks-critic-1 (test-tasks): Preamble structural-check scope excludes denial message content scenarios
findings.match_reason: GAP-44 introduced both the preamble and the distinct category label. The finding identifies scope ambiguity GAP-44 left unresolved.

findings.finding: TASK-INFRA-1: Structural denial verification preamble does not clearly cover denial message content scenarios
findings.match_reason: Same concern as test-tasks finding. GAP-44 preamble label matches group 5-7 only, not 8-9.

findings.finding: INT-deny-response field names conflict with verification preamble field references
findings.match_reason: GAP-44 outcome describes flat-field preamble but INT-deny-response uses hookSpecificOutput nesting. No existing gap addresses this inconsistency.

findings.finding: INT-deny-response lacks a JSON shape, leaving nesting structure implicit
findings.match_reason: GAP-15 created the interface with field descriptions but no concrete JSON envelope example.

findings.finding: Component responsibility omits the tool-type routing mechanism
findings.match_reason: GAP-37 covers field extraction. GAP-40 added routing note in infra only. Finding concerns placement in technical section.

findings.finding: INT-deny-response lacks a concrete JSON structure example
findings.match_reason: Semantically identical to Finding 2. GAP-15 resolved at field-enumeration level, not JSON envelope level.

findings.finding: SCN-denial-grep Given uses imprecise not set instead of established taxonomy language
findings.match_reason: GAP-47 narrowed SCN-bypass-not-set but SCN-denial-grep added by GAP-38 uses imprecise language.

findings.finding: Deny JSON wire format in infra conflicts with INT-deny-response interface contract
findings.match_reason: Current infra and INT-deny-response are structurally consistent. Flat-form language appears only in resolved.md outcome text.

findings.finding: hookEventName value not verified despite INT-deny-response specifying Always PreToolUse
findings.match_reason: No existing gap covers the missing hookEventName value assertion.

findings.finding: TSK-verify-bypass checks hookEventName presence but not its value
findings.match_reason: Semantically identical to Finding 7. No existing gap covers hookEventName value check omission.

findings.finding: technical-1: Context vocabulary "tool input path" conflicts with CMP multi-field extraction
findings.match_reason: No existing gap addresses the context section establishing 'tool input path' as a single, unified concept while the CMP specifies three distinct input fields. GAP-37 outcome says 'Changed responsibility from Pattern-match tool input paths against protected file names to Extract target path from tool-specific input fields' — it updated the CMP responsibility but left the context vocabulary unchanged. GAP-57 and GAP-69 similarly expanded the CMP routing description without revisiting the context abstraction.

findings.finding: technical-2: CMP responsibility prescribes routing detection mechanism, constraining implementation
findings.match_reason: No existing gap questions whether the '(not tool_name)' parenthetical belongs at the responsibility level rather than the decisions section. GAP-57 deliberately introduced this language: its decision says 'Expand the CMP-block-skill-internals extraction responsibility to integrate the routing mechanism: Determine tool type via tool_input key presence (not tool_name).' The finding argues 'the assignable statement is route to the correct input field based on tool type; the mechanism for detecting tool type is a developer decision' — a placement critique that was never raised before GAP-57's resolution.

findings.finding: technical-critic-1: `CMP-block-skill-internals` claims no dependencies while the routing mechanism requires JSON parsing
findings.match_reason: Covered by DEPENDENCY-1 (same batch); DEPENDENCY-1 is more comprehensive. This finding says 'The component lists `dependencies: []`, leaving no documentation of how this is technically feasible' and 'GAP-51 already acknowledged jq as a documented project dependency, yet the component dependency list was never updated.' DEPENDENCY-1 says 'declares `dependencies: []` (line 38) but its core responsibility requires JSON parsing' and additionally raises the missing Y-statement angle ('no Y-statement documents the decision to use jq for JSON parsing over alternatives') and cites the writing-technical-design dependency guideline. DEPENDENCY-1 subsumes this finding entirely.

findings.finding: DEPENDENCY-1: CMP-block-skill-internals declares empty dependencies despite requiring `jq` for JSON parsing
findings.match_reason: No existing gap addresses the empty `dependencies: []` in the component definition. GAP-51 decision says 'Name jq as the JSON inspection tool in the manual-test-procedure verification instructions' — its outcome 'Named jq as the JSON inspection tool in manual-test-procedure verification instructions,' adding jq only to the infra testing procedure. No gap raises the concern that the component dependency list itself should list jq, nor that a Y-statement for the jq-over-alternatives choice is missing from the Decisions section.

findings.finding: technical-critic-1: `CAP-actionable-denial` has no corresponding technical objective
findings.match_reason: No existing gap addresses the asymmetry between the two functional capabilities and the two technical objectives. GAP-6 outcome says 'Renamed OBJ-zero-state to OBJ-session-bypass and OBJ-minimal-diff to OBJ-self-contained' — it renamed existing objectives but did not add one for CAP-actionable-denial. The finding notes 'GAP-6 renamed existing objectives, not added one for this capability' and identifies that 'CAP-actionable-denial... is addressed only through INT-deny-response MUST/MUST NOT constraints and a CMP responsibility line. It has no OBJ-* slug.'

findings.finding: requirements-coverage-1: Denial message content verified for Glob only; Read and Grep denials unverified
findings.match_reason: No existing gap addresses per-tool-type scenario coverage of denial message content. GAP-19 deferred 'individual deny JSON field coverage beyond systemMessage' (i.e., permissionDecisionReason content) — a different axis. GAP-35 outcome says 'Updated CHG-denial-message from When a skill file read is denied to When skill file access is denied,' making the wording tool-agnostic but not addressing scenario coverage across tool paths. The finding says 'A tool-specific regression in the denial message — e.g., the Read code path retaining stale plugin-disable advice — would pass all scenarios undetected,' a coverage concern unraised in any prior gap.

findings.finding: infra-1: jq example commands cover only two of four required denial assertions
findings.match_reason: No existing gap addresses the infra jq example commands being incomplete relative to the four prose-listed assertions. GAP-51 outcome says 'Named jq as the JSON inspection tool in manual-test-procedure verification instructions,' establishing the two example commands (permissionDecision and systemMessage fields). GAP-59 outcome says 'Extended the deny verification preamble in TSK-verify-bypass to assert hookSpecificOutput.hookEventName equals PreToolUse' — updating the task section only. The finding says 'GAP-59 added the hookEventName assertion to TSK-verify-bypass but the infra jq examples were not updated,' a divergence no gap tracks.

findings.finding: tasks-critic-1: No content verification for permissionDecisionReason
findings.match_reason: GAP-19 description says 'REQ-updated-denial-message only asserts on systemMessage content — it has no rule or then-step verifying the permissionDecisionReason field is also updated'; GAP-19 decision says 'Record as out-of-scope: individual deny JSON field coverage beyond systemMessage is deferred.' The finding says 'TSK-verify-bypass verifies that hookSpecificOutput.permissionDecisionReason is present but has no scenario checking its content' and 'An implementer could copy an old value... and pass all ten scenarios while violating both the task requirement and the interface spec.' The core concern — no content assertion for permissionDecisionReason — was explicitly raised and deferred by GAP-19, with GAP-36 reinforcing: 'No MUST constraints added — aligns with GAP-19s established deferral of field-level normative coverage beyond systemMessage.'
